tasks.instruction: Przeczytaj ponizszy tekst. Zdecyduj, ktore zdania (1.1-1.8) sa zgodne z trescia tekstu (T – True), ktore sa niezgodne (F – False), a ktore zawieraja informacje niepodane w tekscie (NI – No Information).
tasks.text:
SPACE EXPLORATION

Humans have always been fascinated by the stars and planets. However, it was not until the 20th century that we developed the technology to actually travel into space. The Space Age began on 4 October 1957, when the Soviet Union launched Sputnik 1, the first artificial satellite to orbit the Earth. Sputnik was a small metal ball, only about 58 centimetres in diameter, but its launch changed the world forever.

The race to explore space quickly became a competition between the Soviet Union and the United States, known as the Space Race. The Soviets achieved another historic first in 1961, when cosmonaut Yuri Gagarin became the first human to travel into space. His flight aboard the Vostok 1 spacecraft lasted just 108 minutes. The Americans responded by setting an ambitious goal: to land a man on the Moon before the end of the 1960s.

On 20 July 1969, American astronauts Neil Armstrong and Buzz Aldrin became the first humans to walk on the Moon during the Apollo 11 mission. A third astronaut, Michael Collins, remained in orbit around the Moon while his colleagues explored the surface. Armstrong famously said, "That's one small step for man, one giant leap for mankind," as he stepped onto the lunar surface. In total, twelve astronauts walked on the Moon between 1969 and 1972.

After the Moon landings, the focus of space exploration shifted to building space stations and sending robotic probes to other planets. The International Space Station (ISS), launched in 1998, orbits the Earth at an altitude of about 400 kilometres and travels at a speed of roughly 28,000 kilometres per hour. Astronauts from many different countries live and work on the ISS, conducting scientific experiments in the unique environment of microgravity.

Today, both government space agencies and private companies are planning new missions. Several organisations are developing plans to send astronauts to Mars, which would be the longest and most difficult crewed space mission ever attempted. Mars is, on average, about 225 million kilometres from Earth, and a round trip could take up to three years.

items.statement: Sputnik 1 was launched by the United States.
Yuri Gagarin's first space flight lasted less than two hours.
Gagarin was awarded a special medal by the Soviet government after his flight.
Three astronauts walked on the Moon during the Apollo 11 mission.
Twelve people in total have walked on the Moon.
The ISS travels at approximately 28,000 kilometres per hour.
The ISS was built entirely by the United States and Russia.
Mars is, on average, about 150 million kilometres from Earth.
